llm_setup.overview: LangChain's ChatOllama is the LLM interface for this system. It wraps the local Ollama server and implements LangChain's BaseChatModel interface, meaning it works with every LangGraph agent pattern, tool binding, and chain without any custom provider code. There is no custom BaseProvider or OllamaProvider class in this architecture.

docs_reference.mcp_note: Phase 0 Step 4 covers this — use confirmed results, do not re-query.

installation.note: Do NOT install langchain-community for Ollama — langchain-ollama is the dedicated package.

get_llm_factory.mcp_note: Phase 0 Step 4 covers this — use confirmed results, do not re-query.
get_llm_factory.structure: File: backend/app/llm.py - Import ChatOllama from langchain_ollama [verify exact import path via MCP] - def get_llm(model: str | None = None, temperature: float | None = None) -> ChatOllama: """Return a configured ChatOllama instance. Use for every graph node.""" return ChatOllama( model = model or settings.default_model, base_url = settings.ollama_base_url, temperature = temperature if temperature is not None else settings.llm_temperature, [CONTEXT_WINDOW_PARAM] = settings.ollama_num_ctx, [confirm param name via MCP] [KEEP_ALIVE_PARAM] = settings.ollama_keep_alive, [confirm param name via MCP] ) This is the ONLY place in the codebase that constructs ChatOllama.

key_parameters.param: Ollama model name — bare name only, no prefix (e.g., "llama3.2" not "ollama/llama3.2")
0.0 for deterministic routing/classification, 0.7 for creative tasks
Context window in tokens. Ollama default 2048 is too small — use 4096+
How long model stays loaded between calls ("5m", "1h", "-1" = forever)

hardware_requirements.gpu_note: 8GB VRAM = 7B model fully on-GPU (fast). Run `ollama ps` to see memory usage.
hardware_requirements.cold_start_note: First request loads model from disk: 30–90s. Use warm-up in lifespan (see 8_code_generation.xml).

model_selection_guidance.guidance: All model names come from config.py Settings — never hardcode in graph/agent files.

task_model_mapping.mapping: phi4:mini or gemma2:2b — fast, low RAM, sufficient for routing decisions
llama3.2 or qwen2.5:7b — best quality/speed balance

llm_setup.config_fields:
# Add to backend/app/config.py Settings class:
# Ollama / LLM connection ollama_base_url: str = "http://localhost:11434" default_model: str = "llama3.2" llm_temperature: float = 0.7
# Per-agent model overrides (add one per agent from implementation.md) # supervisor_model: str = "phi4:mini" # research_model: str = "llama3.2"
# Ollama performance ollama_num_ctx: int = 4096 # Context window — increase for long conversations ollama_keep_alive: str = "5m" # Keep model in memory between calls (-1 = forever)
llm_setup.availability_check:
# Use in main.py lifespan to verify Ollama is running before starting: import httpx
async def check_ollama(base_url: str) -> bool: try: async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=5.0) as client: r = await client.get(base_url) return r.status_code == 200 except Exception: return False

warm_up_guidance.mcp_note: Phase 0 Step 4 covers this — use confirmed results, do not re-query.
warm_up_guidance.pattern: Add after the Ollama connectivity check in the lifespan startup block. Invoke get_llm() with a minimal test message (e.g., content="ping"). Wrap in try/except — warm-up failure must log a warning but NOT prevent startup. This prevents the 30-90s cold-start that users experience on the very first request. Log success at INFO level: "Model pre-loaded: {settings.default_model}"

modelfile_pattern.description: For agents that need specific parameters baked in, use Ollama Modelfiles. This is optional — ChatOllama parameters (num_ctx, temperature, keep_alive) cover most cases.
modelfile_pattern.example:
# ollama/modelfiles/research_agent.Modelfile FROM llama3.2
SYSTEM """ You are a research specialist. Your only purpose is to find and summarise factual information. You always cite your sources. You never speculate beyond the provided information. """
PARAMETER temperature 0.3 PARAMETER num_predict 1024 PARAMETER num_ctx 4096 PARAMETER keep_alive 5m
modelfile_pattern.when_to_use: Use when the system prompt is large and stable. Passing system_prompt in ChatOllama messages is sufficient for most agents.

error_handling.rule: If Ollama is not running, ChatOllama will throw a connection error on first call — check availability in lifespan and log a warning
Model not found: ChatOllama throws OllamaError — add `validate_model_on_init=False` to avoid startup failures (check availability separately)
Timeout: set `num_ctx` smaller or use a lighter model — LangChain default timeout is 60s which may be too short for large models
